pattern: Treat every (mouse cell, cat cell, side-to-move) as a game node and solve by retrograde analysis from terminal wins; any position that never resolves to a mouse win is a cat win because an unresolved chase exhausts the move budget.
hints: A position is fully described by the mouse cell, the cat cell, and whose turn it is, since jump reachability only depends on the current cells.
Work backwards from terminal states (mouse on food wins; cat on food or sharing the mouse's cell wins) and propagate winners to predecessors.
On the mover's turn the position is a win for that side if any move reaches a winning child; for the opponent it must hold for every move, so count undecided moves and flip when the count hits zero.

edge_inputs.description: statement example one: mouse escapes on an L-shaped board
statement example two: long jump lets the mouse win
statement example three: one shorter jump and the mouse loses
small grid with a wall splitting paths
cat sits next to the food

stress.description: tiny random boards (at most six floor cells) so the ply-bounded oracle stays fast
stress.note: exactly one M, C, F on distinct floor cells; boards kept tiny for the exponential oracle
